TIM_BASIC_NO_CR2.name: TIM_BASIC_NO_CR2
TIM_BASIC_NO_CR2.description: Virtual Basic timers without CR2 register for common part of TIM_BASIC and TIM_1CH_CMP

TIM_CORE.name: TIM_CORE
TIM_CORE.description: Virtual timer for common part of TIM_BASIC and TIM_1CH

registers.description:
Option register 1
Note: Check Reference Manual to parse this register content

registers.name: OR
registers.description:
Option register 1
Note: Check Reference Manual to parse this register content

variants.name: NotInverted
variants.description: input polarity is not inverted (active low if BKxP = 0, active high if BKxP = 1)

variants.name: Inverted
variants.description: input polarity is inverted (active high if BKxP = 0, active low if BKxP = 1)

variants.name: ActiveLow
variants.description: Break input tim_brk is active low

variants.name: ActiveHigh
variants.description: Break input tim_brk is active high

variants.name: OnCompare
variants.description: CCx DMA request sent when CCx event occurs

variants.name: OnUpdate
variants.description: CCx DMA request sent when update event occurs

variants.name: TI4
variants.description: CCx channel is configured as input, normal mapping: ICx mapped to TIx

variants.name: TI3
variants.description: CCx channel is configured as input, alternate mapping (switches 1 with 2, 3 with 4)

variants.name: TRC
variants.description: CCx channel is configured as input, ICx is mapped on TRC

variants.name: Output
variants.description: CCx channel is configured as output

variants.name: Div1
variants.description: t_DTS = t_CK_INT

variants.name: Div2
variants.description: t_DTS = 2 × t_CK_INT

variants.name: Div4
variants.description: t_DTS = 4 × t_CK_INT

variants.name: EdgeAligned
variants.description: The counter counts up or down depending on the direction bit

variants.name: CenterAligned1
variants.description: The counter counts up and down alternatively. Output compare interrupt flags are set only when the counter is counting down.

variants.name: CenterAligned2
variants.description: The counter counts up and down alternatively. Output compare interrupt flags are set only when the counter is counting up.

variants.name: CenterAligned3
variants.description: The counter counts up and down alternatively. Output compare interrupt flags are set both when the counter is counting up or down.

variants.name: Up
variants.description: Counter used as upcounter

variants.name: Down
variants.description: Counter used as downcounter

variants.name: NotInverted
variants.description: ETR is noninverted, active at high level or rising edge

variants.name: Inverted
variants.description: ETR is inverted, active at low level or falling edge

variants.name: Div1
variants.description: Prescaler OFF

variants.name: Div2
variants.description: ETRP frequency divided by 2

variants.name: Div4
variants.description: ETRP frequency divided by 4

variants.name: Div8
variants.description: ETRP frequency divided by 8

variants.name: NoFilter
variants.description: No filter, sampling is done at fDTS

variants.name: FCK_INT_N2
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fCK_INT, N=2

variants.name: FCK_INT_N4
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fCK_INT, N=4

variants.name: FCK_INT_N8
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fCK_INT, N=8

variants.name: FDTS_Div2_N6
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/2, N=6

variants.name: FDTS_Div2_N8
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/2, N=8

variants.name: FDTS_Div4_N6
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/4, N=6

variants.name: FDTS_Div4_N8
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/4, N=8

variants.name: FDTS_Div8_N6
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/8, N=6

variants.name: FDTS_Div8_N8
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/8, N=8

variants.name: FDTS_Div16_N5
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/16, N=5

variants.name: FDTS_Div16_N6
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/16, N=6

variants.name: FDTS_Div16_N8
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/16, N=8

variants.name: FDTS_Div32_N5
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/32, N=5

variants.name: FDTS_Div32_N6
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/32, N=6

variants.name: FDTS_Div32_N8
variants.description: fSAMPLING=fDTS/32, N=8

variants.name: NoEffect
variants.description: No effect of TIM_OC5REF on TIM_OCxREFC (x=1-3)

variants.name: LogicalAND
variants.description: TIM_OCxREFC is the logical AND of TIM_OCxREF and TIM_OC5REF

variants.name: Disabled
variants.description: No bit is write protected

variants.name: Level1
variants.description: DTG bits in TIMx_BDTR register, OISx and OISxN bits in TIMx_CR2 register and BKBID/BKE/BKP/AOE bits in TIMx_BDTR register can no longer be written

variants.name: Level2
variants.description: LOCK Level 1 + CC Polarity bits (CCxP/CCxNP bits in TIMx_CCER register, as long as the related channel is configured in output through the CCxS bits) as well as OSSR and OSSI bits can no longer be written.

variants.name: Level3
variants.description: LOCK Level 2 + CC Control bits (OCxM and OCxPE bits in TIMx_CCMRx registers, as long as the related channel is configured in output through the CCxS bits) can no longer be written.

variants.name: Reset
variants.description: The UG bit from the TIMx_EGR register is used as trigger output

variants.name: Enable
variants.description: The counter enable signal, CNT_EN, is used as trigger output

variants.name: Update
variants.description: The update event is selected as trigger output

variants.name: ComparePulse
variants.description: The trigger output send a positive pulse when the CC1IF flag it to be set, as soon as a capture or a compare match occurred

variants.name: CompareOC1
variants.description: OC1REF signal is used as trigger output

variants.name: CompareOC2
variants.description: OC2REF signal is used as trigger output

variants.name: CompareOC3
variants.description: OC3REF signal is used as trigger output

variants.name: CompareOC4
variants.description: OC4REF signal is used as trigger output

variants.name: Reset
variants.description: The UG bit from the TIMx_EGR register is used as TRGO2

variants.name: Enable
variants.description: The counter enable signal, CNT_EN, is used as TRGO2

variants.name: Update
variants.description: The update event is selected as TRGO2

variants.name: ComparePulse
variants.description: TRGO2 send a positive pulse when the CC1IF flag it to be set, as soon as a capture or a compare match occurred

variants.name: CompareOC1
variants.description: OC1REF signal is used as TRGO2

variants.name: CompareOC2
variants.description: OC2REF signal is used as TRGO2

variants.name: CompareOC3
variants.description: OC3REF signal is used as TRGO2

variants.name: CompareOC4
variants.description: OC4REF signal is used as TRGO2

variants.name: CompareOC5
variants.description: OC5REF signal is used as TRGO2

variants.name: CompareOC6
variants.description: OC6REF signal is used as TRGO2

variants.name: ComparePulse_OC4
variants.description: OC4REF rising or falling edges generate pulses on TRGO2

variants.name: ComparePulse_OC6
variants.description: OC6REF rising or falling edges generate pulses on TRGO2

variants.name: ComparePulse_OC4_Or_OC6_Rising
variants.description: OC4REF or OC6REF rising edges generate pulses on TRGO2

variants.name: ComparePulse_OC4_Rising_Or_OC6_Falling
variants.description: OC4REF rising or OC6REF falling edges generate pulses on TRGO2

variants.name: ComparePulse_OC5_Or_OC6_Rising
variants.description: OC5REF or OC6REF rising edges generate pulses on TRGO2

variants.name: ComparePulse_OC5_Rising_Or_OC6_Falling
variants.description: OC5REF rising or OC6REF falling edges generate pulses on TRGO2

variants.name: NoSync
variants.description: No action

variants.name: Sync
variants.description: The effect of an event on the trigger input (TRGI) is delayed to allow a perfect synchronization between the current timer and its slaves (through TRGO). It is useful if we want to synchronize several timers on a single external event.

variants.name: Frozen
variants.description: The comparison between the output compare register TIMx_CCRy and the counter TIMx_CNT has no effect on the outputs

variants.name: ActiveOnMatch
variants.description: Set channel to active level on match. OCyREF signal is forced high when the counter matches the capture/compare register

variants.name: InactiveOnMatch
variants.description: Set channel to inactive level on match. OCyREF signal is forced low when the counter matches the capture/compare register

variants.name: Toggle
variants.description: OCyREF toggles when TIMx_CNT=TIMx_CCRy

variants.name: ForceInactive
variants.description: OCyREF is forced low

variants.name: ForceActive
variants.description: OCyREF is forced high

variants.name: PwmMode1
variants.description: In upcounting, channel is active as long as TIMx_CNT<TIMx_CCRy else inactive. In downcounting, channel is inactive as long as TIMx_CNT>TIMx_CCRy else active

variants.name: PwmMode2
variants.description: Inversely to PwmMode1

variants.name: Disabled
variants.description: When inactive, OC/OCN outputs are disabled

variants.name: IdleLevel
variants.description: When inactive, OC/OCN outputs are forced to idle level

variants.name: Disabled
variants.description: When inactive, OC/OCN outputs are disabled

variants.name: IdleLevel
variants.description: When inactive, OC/OCN outputs are enabled with their inactive level

variants.name: Disabled
variants.description: Slave mode disabled - if CEN = '1' then the prescaler is clocked directly by the internal clock.

variants.name: Encoder_Mode_1
variants.description: Encoder mode 1 - Counter counts up/down on TI2FP1 edge depending on TI1FP2 level.

variants.name: Encoder_Mode_2
variants.description: Encoder mode 2 - Counter counts up/down on TI1FP2 edge depending on TI2FP1 level.

variants.name: Encoder_Mode_3
variants.description: Encoder mode 3 - Counter counts up/down on both TI1FP1 and TI2FP2 edges depending on the level of the other input.

variants.name: Reset_Mode
variants.description: Reset Mode - Rising edge of the selected trigger input (TRGI) reinitializes the counter and generates an update of the registers.

variants.name: Gated_Mode
variants.description: Gated Mode - The counter clock is enabled when the trigger input (TRGI) is high. The counter stops (but is not reset) as soon as the trigger becomes low. Both start and stop of the counter are controlled.

variants.name: Trigger_Mode
variants.description: Trigger Mode - The counter starts at a rising edge of the trigger TRGI (but it is not reset). Only the start of the counter is controlled.

variants.name: Ext_Clock_Mode
variants.description: External Clock Mode 1 - Rising edges of the selected trigger (TRGI) clock the counter.

variants.name: Combined_Reset_Trigger
variants.description: Rising edge of the selected trigger input (tim_trgi) reinitializes the counter, generates an update of the registers and starts the counter.

variants.name: Normal
variants.description: The TIMx_CH1 pin is connected to TI1 input

variants.name: XOR
variants.description: The TIMx_CH1, CH2, CH3 pins are connected to TI1 input

variants.name: ITR0
variants.description: Internal Trigger 0

variants.name: ITR1
variants.description: Internal Trigger 1

variants.name: ITR2
variants.description: Internal Trigger 2

variants.name: ITR3
variants.description: Internal Trigger 3

variants.name: TI1F_ED
variants.description: TI1 Edge Detector

variants.name: TI1FP1
variants.description: Filtered Timer Input 1

variants.name: TI2FP2
variants.description: Filtered Timer Input 2

variants.name: ETRF
variants.description: External Trigger input

variants.name: ITR4
variants.description: Internal Trigger 4

variants.name: ITR5
variants.description: Internal Trigger 5

variants.name: ITR6
variants.description: Internal Trigger 6

variants.name: ITR7
variants.description: Internal Trigger 7

variants.name: ITR8
variants.description: Internal Trigger 8

variants.name: ITR9
variants.description: Internal Trigger 9

variants.name: ITR10
variants.description: Internal Trigger 10

variants.name: ITR11
variants.description: Internal Trigger 11

variants.name: ITR12
variants.description: Internal Trigger 12

variants.name: ITR13
variants.description: Internal Trigger 13

variants.name: ITR14
variants.description: Internal Trigger 14

variants.name: ITR15
variants.description: Internal Trigger 15

variants.name: AnyEvent
variants.description: Any of counter overflow/underflow, setting UG, or update through slave mode, generates an update interrupt or DMA request

variants.name: CounterOnly
variants.description: Only counter overflow/underflow generates an update interrupt or DMA request
